talk_metadata.session_title: Academic Entrepreneurship and Getting Discoveries into Patients, part one: Liftoff
talk_metadata.talk_title: Career Journey from Academia to Chief Commercialization Officer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: 'I had no idea what comes next. What do you do once you have your patent?'
Speaker said: 'I was all surprised again. That was another moment in my career that I was like, "What do you mean I cannot develop in this target, or this is not commercially viable?"'

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: The speaker noted they were 'unsure what was the mechanism of action' despite finding it transactivated EGFR and HER2, and the project did not progress to a commercial product.

key_data_gaps: No clinical data presented for the substance P antibody

crispro_opportunity.description: The speaker's early failure with substance P antibodies due to mechanistic uncertainty suggests a need for high-resolution mapping of neuropeptide-driven EGFR/HER2 transactivation.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: We're unsure what was the mechanism of action, but we found that it was transactivating EGFR and HER2.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine could be used to map the specific interactome of substance P to identify the precise transactivation mechanism and identify more druggable nodes than the neuropeptide itself.

cited_competitors.name: Vertex Pharmaceuticals
cited_competitors.context: Former employer where speaker ran translational oncology

cited_competitors.name: Eisai Pharmaceuticals
cited_competitors.context: Former employer for portfolio strategy in neurology/Alzheimer's

cited_competitors.name: Danaher Corporation
cited_competitors.context: Former employer as head of innovation

unresolved_questions: What is the exact mechanism of action by which substance P transactivates EGFR and HER2?

talk_metadata.session_title: Academic Entrepreneurship and Getting Discoveries into Patients, part one: Liftoff
talk_metadata.talk_title: Perspectives on Signal Transduction and Drug Discovery
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: 'I can't tell you how much money we spent and never made a drug that was selective enough to go forward to hit this target.'

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of selectivity for the target kinase led to failure despite high activity in glioblastoma.
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker admitted they 'never made a drug that was selective enough to go forward', implying off-target toxicity or lack of potency.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Failure of the MDR1 inhibitor in the clinic despite a successful pharmacophore design.
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: VX-701, which of course also didn't make it in the clinic.

key_data_gaps: No specific clinical failure data for VX-701 presented

crispro_opportunity.description: The failure of eEF2 kinase inhibitors due to selectivity issues presents an opportunity to use precision targeting or PROTACs to achieve the selectivity J&J could not.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: never made a drug that was selective enough to go forward to hit this target.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO could identify specific allosteric sites or degradation tags to achieve the selectivity required for eEF2 inhibition in glioblastoma.

cited_competitors.name: Johnson & Johnson
cited_competitors.context: Former employer where speaker headed R&D

unresolved_questions: Why was the eEF2 kinase target not druggable with sufficient selectivity?

talk_metadata.session_title: Academic Entrepreneurship and Getting Discoveries into Patients, part one: Liftoff

crispro_opportunity.description: The speaker highlights the critical nature of the pre-IND and 'interact' meetings to avoid clinical holds, particularly in cell therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: If your IND goes in and the FDA says, "We're going to put it on a hold," which really just means right now that we see a safety issue here... That's a big no-no from a business aspect of it.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's platform can provide the high-resolution non-clinical safety data (in vitro/in vivo) required to satisfy FDA CMC and safety requirements, reducing the risk of clinical holds.

cited_competitors.name: Biogen
cited_competitors.context: Former employer working on expressed proteins

cited_competitors.name: Sanofi
cited_competitors.context: Former employer working on mAb portfolio

talk_metadata.session_title: Academic Entrepreneurship and Getting Discoveries into Patients, part one: Liftoff
talk_metadata.talk_title: Building the Bridge from Academia to Clinic

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: The speaker mentions the company has had 'ups and downs' and a significant reduction in staff (60 to 12), suggesting instability in the asset's development or funding.
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: It's been up and down, tops and turvies, all sorts of things, but we're still plugging away.

key_data_gaps: No specific data presented for the CAR-NK company

crispro_opportunity.description: The speaker's focus on NK cells and the transition to in vivo CAR-NK suggests a need for better targeting and delivery mechanisms.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: We're now into in vivo, CAR T, CAR NK.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine can optimize the targeting ligands for in vivo CAR-NK delivery to increase specificity and reduce off-target toxicity.
